com.pax.poslink.fullIntegration

    Some configuration for Terminal

    • Method Detail

      • getEmvKernelConfigurationSelection

        public java.lang.String getEmvKernelConfigurationSelection()

      • setEmvKernelConfigurationSelection

        public void setEmvKernelConfigurationSelection(java.lang.String emvKernelConfigurationSelection)
        Please refer to EMV Kernel Configuration Type.

        See EMV-KERNEL-CONFIGURATION-TYPE for definition.

        Please note that this is only valid for EMV contact transaction during authorize card.
        Parameters:
        emvKernelConfigurationSelection - EMV kernel configuration selection.

      • getTransactionDate

        public java.lang.String getTransactionDate()

      • setTransactionDate

        public void setTransactionDate(java.lang.String transactionDate)
        9A: Transaction Date Format is YYMMDD.
        Parameters:
        transactionDate - Use terminal date by default

      • getTransactionTime

        public java.lang.String getTransactionTime()

      • setTransactionTime

        public void setTransactionTime(java.lang.String transactionTime)
        9F21: Transaction Time format is HHMMSS.
        Parameters:
        transactionTime - use terminal time by default

      • getCurrencyCode

        public java.lang.String getCurrencyCode()

      • setCurrencyCode

        public void setCurrencyCode(java.lang.String currencyCode)
        5F2A: Transaction Currency Code
        Parameters:
        currencyCode - “840” by default

      • getCurrencyExponent

        public java.lang.String getCurrencyExponent()

      • setCurrencyExponent

        public void setCurrencyExponent(java.lang.String currencyExponent)
        5F36: Transaction Currency Exponent
        Parameters:
        currencyExponent - "02" by default

      • setMerchantCategoryCode

        public void setMerchantCategoryCode(java.lang.String merchantCategoryCode)
        A Merchant Category Code (MCC) is a four-digit number listed in ISO 18245 for retail financial services. MCC is used to classify the business by the type of goods or services it provides. MCCs are assigned by merchant type (e.g. one for hotels, one for office supply stores, etc.) or by merchant name (e.g. 3000 for United Airlines)

        This field can be set for Contact EMV Chip transactions in the AuthorizeCard command and for Contactless transactions in the InputAccountWithEmv command.

        9F15: Merchant Category Code. ("0001" by default)
        Parameters:
        merchantCategoryCode - The MCC

      • getMerchantCategoryCode

        public java.lang.String getMerchantCategoryCode()

      • setTransactionSequenceNumber

        public void setTransactionSequenceNumber(java.lang.String transactionSequenceNumber)
        9F41: Transaction Sequence Number. Counter maintained by the terminal that is incremented by one for each transaction
        Parameters:
        transactionSequenceNumber -

      • getTransactionSequenceNumber

        public java.lang.String getTransactionSequenceNumber()

      • getTransactionCVMLimit

        public java.lang.String getTransactionCVMLimit()

      • setTransactionCVMLimit

        public void setTransactionCVMLimit(java.lang.String transactionCVMLimit)
        Configure the limit value of CVM for current transaction. (Only for Visa payWave and only for InputAccount)
        Parameters:
        transactionCVMLimit -
        Since:
        V1.07.00
